evals.prompt: Ingest every markdown document under docs/ into the semantic corpus so we can search them, and tell me what you ingested.
evals.expected_output: Ingests each MIF doc via ingest_mif_document (or mif-cli ingest), skips the ADR documents under docs/adr/ (the docs carrying a top-level description: key) with the stated round-trip-drift reason, and reports the ingested count plus the skipped files.

deterministic_checks.description: states the round-trip-drift reason for the ADR skip
reports that ADR documents were skipped rather than failing silently

expectations: Skips the ADR documents under docs/adr/ in the bulk ingest, identified by their top-level description: key or path (not by a type: adr value - the repo's ADRs carry type: semantic), and states the Rust round-trip-drift reason rather than letting them fail one by one
Warns that the first ingest downloads the embedding model (network, slower) before running a bulk pass
Reports ingest as fail-closed: a doc that fails validation or round-trip stores nothing, and any such failure is reported per-file, not hidden

evals.prompt: Which existing doc in this repo covers validating a document against the MIF schema? Search the corpus semantically.
evals.expected_output: Runs search_documents (or mif-cli search) with a natural-language query against .mif/vectors.db and presents the ranked (score, id) results, identifying the top match.
expectations: Presents ranked results with their cosine scores and ids verbatim from the tool output, without inventing or reordering matches
Frames the result as a discovery candidate, not a validation verdict — conformance questions still go to mif-validate

evals.prompt: I just wrote docs/reference/new-page.md and ingested it. What should its relationships[] link to? Use the corpus.
evals.expected_output: Runs find_similar_documents (or mif-cli find-similar) with the document's urn:mif id and offers the top matches as candidate relationships[] targets for the author to accept.
expectations: Uses the document's urn:mif:<id> (from frontmatter id) as the anchor and notes the anchor itself is excluded from results
Offers the matches as candidates the author accepts or rejects — does not silently write relationships[] into the doc as if similarity were authoritative

evals.prompt: Search the corpus for docs about release attestation.
evals.expected_output: Detects that neither an mif-mcp MCP server nor a mif-cli binary is available, states the corpus layer is unavailable, and points at the install route without fabricating results.

deterministic_checks.description: states the corpus layer is unavailable

expectations: States plainly that the optional mif-rs tooling is not installed and that the rest of the plugin is unaffected
Points to the install route (attested release binaries from modeled-information-format/mif-rs, or cargo install) instead of substituting grep output presented as semantic search

evals.prompt: How big is our semantic corpus right now, and where does it live?
evals.expected_output: Runs corpus_stats (or mif-cli corpus-stats) and reports the document count, embedding dimension, and the .mif/vectors.db path.
expectations: Reports count, dim, and db path from the tool output verbatim
Notes the store is a gitignored local derived index, safe to delete and rebuild by re-ingesting
